data.description:
# Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7) + fail-open tool wrappers

## Why
The deterministic floor is the only tier that BLOCKS, so it must be sound (near-zero FP) and must generalize across the diverse client base via fail-open tooling — otherwise the gate can't ship polyglot.

## What
The P1-P7 deterministic checks plus the external-tool wrappers, all fail-open.

## Scope
- P1 readiness shape; P2 file/symbol/import resolution; P3 package existence; P4 oversize signals; P5 task-DAG validity; P6 AC/DD quality; P7 destructive sniff (escalation only).
- Tool wrappers (NEW integrations, none currently in repo): solidlsp (reachable via the configured Serena MCP server; to be wired, not a rebar library) + ast-grep; syft + per-ecosystem registry + LLM-fallback; scc + lizard.
- Fail-open IRONCLAD: unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout -> skipped = PASS, never FAIL; coverage recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P1-P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, each emitting PASS/FAIL/skipped with a cited reason.
- [ ] Each tool wrapper degrades to skipped=PASS on unsupported-lang / no-server / crash / timeout, proven by a fixture test per failure mode (in src/rebar/...).
- [ ] Hard "missing" verdicts come only from a top-tier server; weak/absent tooling yields could-not-confirm (skipped), never FAIL.
- [ ] Coverage (which checks ran vs skipped) is recorded for the attestation.
data.title: Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7) + fail-open tool wrappers
